evals.prompt: Aqui estão os tokens do nosso Design System exportados pelo plugin do Figma. A pasta é tokens-json/plugin/. Converta tudo para CSS Custom Properties e organize os arquivos conforme a estrutura de saída definida. Inclui as foundations, os temas light e dark, e os estilos de tipografia e efeitos.
evals.expected_output: CSS files generated under src/styles/tokens/ with: brand.css, foundation.css, typography.css (including .text-* classes), effects.css (including .shadow-* classes), layout files, grid files, theme.light.css (:root), theme.dark.css ([data-theme='dark']), component files, quasar-overrides.css, and index.css importing all in correct order. All token references like {color.gray.950} should be resolved to var(--color-gray-950). Variable names should follow the JSON path exactly.

evals.prompt: Atualizei os tokens no Figma e exportei os JSONs novamente. Os arquivos atualizados estão em tokens-json/plugin/. Faz o sync com o CSS que já gerou antes e me mostra o diff antes de aplicar.
evals.expected_output: Agent reads both the existing CSS files and the new JSON tokens, computes a diff showing added/changed/removed tokens clearly, asks for user confirmation before applying, then updates only the affected CSS files. The diff format should be: ✅ Added, 🔄 Changed, ❌ Removed with token names and values.

evals.prompt: Quero aplicar os tokens do Design System no componente QBtn do Quasar. Temos os botões em variantes: filled (usa a cor primária), secondary (fundo transparente com borda) e tertiary (apenas texto). Como fica o CSS de override?
evals.expected_output: A CSS override file (or additions to quasar-overrides.css) that maps design system component tokens (--button-secondary-enabled, --button-secondary-hovered, --button-secondary-pressed) to the QBtn CSS selectors. Should use .q-btn classes, not Vue component wrappers. Should handle all three variants and :hover/:active states. Should also correctly set --q-primary to the design system primary color.
